title: Set DASHBOARD_PASSWORD from default change-me to a real password
description: The .env file still has DASHBOARD_PASSWORD=change-me (the shipped default). Anyone on the LAN can access the dashboard. User confirmed they never set a password. Needs to be changed to a real secret before the dashboard is considered secure.

title: Backend: ingest endpoints + swap reads to Postgres (server.js)
description: Add POST /api/ingest/sessions (device auth) -> db.upsertSessions + SSE emit. Swap every sessionManager.getAllSessions() read to db.getAllSessions({device}). Add ?device filter and GET /api/devices. /api/active uses end_time/updated_at. Remove local watcher from server. Keep aggregation bodies verbatim.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: Collector agent (collector.js)
description: Per-device agent: load collector.config.json, run SessionManager + chokidar watcher (lifted from server.js), push changed sessions to backend /api/ingest/sessions with device key. Debounce + delta by mtime. collector.config.example.json template, npm run collector script.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: Auth: device-key ingest + dashboard login (lib/auth.js)
description: deviceAuth middleware (Bearer key, hashed verify) for /api/ingest/*; dashboardAuth middleware (signed cookie) guarding static + read APIs; POST /api/login; scripts/register-device.js to register a device and print key once.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: DB layer: Postgres schema + lib/db.js
description: Add db/schema.sql (devices, sessions JSONB, session_meta) and lib/db.js (pg Pool wrapper: upsertDevice/touchDevice/getDevices, upsertSessions, getAllSessions({device}) returning existing session shape, setMeta/getMeta, verifyDeviceKey). docker-compose.yml for postgres:16, db:migrate script.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: EPIC: LAN-shared persistent multi-device backend
description: Move Mission Control from single-machine, file-read, in-memory to a LAN-hosted Postgres-backed backend that aggregates sessions/cost/tokens/projects from multiple devices, filterable by device, with device-key ingest auth and dashboard login. See plan: all-the-information-that-quirky-pearl.md
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: Fix usage stats: weekly + 5-hour rolling window periods
description: Claude Code Pro uses two reset cycles: (1) a 5-hour rolling window starting from the first prompt in that window, and (2) a weekly cap that resets on Mondays. The current /api/usage-stats endpoint uses a monthly billing period (billingDay in config.json) which is wrong. Need to: update config.json to replace billingDay with weeklyResetDay, update /api/usage-stats to compute both a weekly period (Mon-Mon) and a 5-hour rolling window, and update the UsageDashboard component to display both periods with correct labels.
close_reason: Implemented weekly period (Mon–Mon) and 5-hour rolling window in server.js + config.json. Frontend updated with 5-hour window card and corrected labels.

title: Usage Dashboard: UsageDashboard React component + view state + top bar
description:
Backend and CSS are complete (commit 09c5cc1). Three things remaining:
1. Add UsageDashboard component to app.js (before App function)
2. Add view state ('sessions' | 'usage') and usageStats state to App
3. Update top bar to be dynamic per-project and include totalTokens stat

Resume point from changelog: add UsageDashboard component before App function, add view/usageStats state, update top bar render to be dynamic and include tokens.
close_reason: UsageDashboard component, view/usageStats state, and dynamic top bar all implemented and verified in browser

title: Update start.sh + missioncontrol alias for v0.2 (Postgres/collector)
description: start.sh still launches the old single-machine flow on :9000. For the new architecture it should: ensure .env exists, docker compose up -d postgres, npm run db:migrate, start the backend, and optionally start the host collector. Also document the multi-device run steps.
close_reason: Rewrote start.sh for v0.2 host stack (Postgres healthcheck wait, migrate, backend, optional host collector); alias unchanged. Updated changelog.

title: Docs: harmonize prd/infra/security/sbom + changelog
description: Resolve Conflict Resolution Matrix: update prd.md (LAN multi-device + auth), infra.md (Postgres/Docker/collector/LAN listener), security.md (device-key + dashboard auth threat model), sbom.md (pg + cookie/session lib + Postgres/Docker), changelog.md.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: Frontend: login screen + device filter (public/)
description: Login form on 401 -> POST /api/login. Device selector in top bar passing ?device= to refreshData fetches. Show device on session rows + per-device sidebar breakdown.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end: Postgres-backed LAN multi-device backend, collector, device-key+dashboard auth, device-filtered frontend, docs harmonized. E2E tested (142 sessions ingested, auth gates, device filter, meta persistence, browser login).

title: Refine dashboard: paginate sessions to 20 + replace monthly-budget bar with token-limit bars
description: Sessions page renders all rows; add show-20 + 'Show more' (+20/click). Replace red 'this month vs monthly budget' bar with weekly + 5-hour token usage/remaining bars. Adds config token caps (weeklyTokenLimit, fiveHourTokenLimit, weeklyResetWeekday), weekly fixed-reset window in /api/usage-stats.
close_reason: Implemented: sessions paginated to 20 with Show more; replaced monthly-budget red bar with weekly + 5-hour token-limit bars (config caps + weekly fixed-reset window). Verified in browser (green + over-limit states).

title: Toolkit tab: scan skills, commands, plugins, and global settings
description: Add a new 'Toolkit' tab to mission-control that scans all projects under scanPath and shows: unique custom skills/commands (deduplicated by name with source project list), installed plugins from ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json, and global ~/.claude/settings.json. Surfaces the user's full Claude Code toolkit across all projects in one view. No MCP servers are currently configured so that section is optional/future.
close_reason: Toolkit tab implemented: toolkitScanner.js, /api/toolkit endpoint, ToolkitPanel component with skills/plugins/global settings sections

title: LAN setup guide page (public/guide.html)
description: Self-contained HTML guide served at /guide.html explaining how to run MISSION-CONTROL on the host and on each LAN device, so any device can open it and see the exact dashboard URL + commands. Served openly via existing express.static (before auth gate).
close_reason: Added public/guide.html served at /guide.html; verified served openly + reachable over LAN IP + renders correctly.

title: Optional TLS for the LAN listener
description: The backend serves plain HTTP on 0.0.0.0:9000; a hostile actor already on the LAN could intercept the dashboard password/cookie. Offer optional self-signed TLS (or document fronting with a reverse proxy / Tailscale) for untrusted networks.
close_reason: Implemented via parallel agents, integrated to master, verified live (toolkit aggregation + ?device, manual restore, TLS boot).

title: Cross-device session restore
description: POST /api/restore/:id is host-only (launches osascript on the host). Support restoring sessions that live on other devices — e.g. relay a restore signal to that device's collector, or surface the resume command for the user to run there.
close_reason: Implemented via parallel agents, integrated to master, verified live (toolkit aggregation + ?device, manual restore, TLS boot).

title: Per-device toolkit ingest
description: GET /api/toolkit currently scans only the backend host's filesystem. For true multi-device parity, have each collector scan its local toolkit (skills/commands/hooks/MCP/plugins/settings) and push it, then aggregate per-device in the dashboard.
close_reason: Implemented via parallel agents, integrated to master, verified live (toolkit aggregation + ?device, manual restore, TLS boot).
